text: MongoDB provides several backup strategies. For sharded clusters, use mongodump and mongorestore, or coordinated backup with Atlas, Cloud Manager, or Ops Manager. File system snapshots can back up each component individually. Limit the cluster balancer to provide a window for regular backup operations.

Best practices for data backups: use file system snapshots when possible for efficiency. For mongodump, stop the balancer and use fsync or db.fsyncLock() to block writes during backups. Sharded clusters can use coordinated backup processes that maintain atomicity across shards.

When an alert condition triggers, Atlas displays a warning symbol and sends notifications. Resolve alerts by fixing the immediate problem, implementing a long-term solution, and monitoring progress. View alerts on the Organization Alerts or Project Alerts page. Acknowledge alerts to pause notifications until the condition resolves.

To resolve alerts: navigate to the Alerts page, select the alert, and click Mark Acknowledge or Acknowledge. For Query Targeting alerts, optimize indexes and fix inefficient queries. For Connection alerts, increase deployment capacity or fix connection pooling issues.

RAG combines retrieval with generation. Embed your documents, store them in MongoDB Atlas with a vector index, then at query time: embed the user question, run vector search to find relevant chunks, and pass the context plus question to an LLM to generate an answer.

Vector search finds semantically similar content. Embed documents at ingest time with input_type document, and embed queries at search time with input_type query. Use the same embedding model for both to ensure vectors live in the same space.

Chunk your documents before embedding. Use RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter or similar: split on paragraph boundaries, keep 1-2 paragraphs per chunk (~200 tokens), use 0 overlap for contextualized embeddings or 15-20% for standard embeddings.

Create a vector search index on your collection with the embedding field path, numDimensions matching your model (e.g. 1024), and similarity metric (cosine, euclidean, or dotProduct). Add filter fields for pre-filtering by metadata.
